<output-file>/Users/steven/.claude/tmp/claude-501/-Users-steven-CoX-mod-Adventure/86fc7165-d979-4827-b088-5359d5c69c61/tasks/w4hii8ofq.output</output-file>

<summary>Dynamic workflow "Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report." completed</summary>
<result>{"question":"CSS grid mesh layouts for mod/card browsers — masonry, bento, asymmetric grids, card density patterns. Specifically for a City of Heroes mod browser with 291 mod cards showing name, category badge, download count, description, and download button. Looking for: modern CSS-only masonry approaches, bento grid patterns, card density toggles, featured/hero card layouts, and any UX research on card grid information hierarchy.","summary":"Synthesis step was skipped or failed — returning 2 verified claims unmerged.","findings":[],"confirmed":[{"claim":"Masonry layout is an experimental technology not yet at Baseline, meaning it does not work in some of the most widely-used browsers and should not be used in production without a fallback.","source":"https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Guides/Grid_layout/Masonry_layout","quote":"This feature is not Baseline because it does not work in some of the most widely-used browsers.","vote":"3-0"},{"claim":"There are two competing specification proposals for CSS masonry as of late 2024: a 'Grid Integrated' option (display: grid; grid-template-rows: masonry) and a 'Grid Independent' option (display: masonry; masonry-template-tracks: ...).","source":"https://webkit.org/blog/16026/css-masonry-syntax/","quote":"Currently, the specification is drafted with two options, going head-to-head — the 'Grid Integrated' option and the 'Grid Independent' option.","vote":"2-0"}],"refuted":[{"claim":"CSS masonry layout uses `display: grid-lanes` (not a `grid-template-rows: masonry` value), as it is defined in Level 3 of the CSS Grid Layout specification under the grid-lanes model.","vote":"1-0","source":"https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Guides/Grid_layout/Masonry_layout"},{"claim":"On the masonry (stacking) axis, items in later rows rise up to fill gaps left by shorter items, rather than staying aligned to a strict grid row.","vote":"1-0","source":"https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Guides/Grid_layout/Masonry_layout"},{"claim":"CSS masonry layout can be implemented using existing grid syntax by adding only one new value to the grid-template-rows or grid-template-columns property, without requiring a new display type.","vote":"0-1","source":"https://webkit.org/blog/16026/css-masonry-syntax/"},{"claim":"Content density is defined as the ratio of information visible compared to the space available in the interface, and two discrete modes are supported: 'comfortable' (default) and 'compact' (for data-intensive views).","vote":"0-0","source":"https://cloudscape.design/foundation/visual-foundation/content-density/"},{"claim":"Comfortable mode must always be set as the default because compact mode can hinder readability, overwhelm users, and prolong content consumption.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://cloudscape.design/foundation/visual-foundation/content-density/"},{"claim":"Content Switcher is recommended for mutually exclusive view states (e.g., list vs. grid), not for filtering or navigation, which constrains its correct use in a mod browser density toggle.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://carbondesignsystem.com/components/content-switcher/usage/"},{"claim":"Card view design guidelines recommend using a gallery grid with built-in CSS responsiveness — cards expand and compress at breakpoints rather than using masonry or bento layouts.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://www.patternfly.org/patterns/card-view/design-guidelines/"},{"claim":"Card density toggle is supported via a toggle between card view and table/list view, not via a within-grid density slider.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://www.patternfly.org/patterns/card-view/design-guidelines/"},{"claim":"Card-based grid layouts consume more vertical space than list views, meaning a given screen cannot show as many cards as it could items in a list view, making density a direct trade-off against visual richness.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://www.nngroup.com/articles/cards-component/"},{"claim":"Cards are not effective for searching or comparing specific items; they work best for browsing heterogeneous or loosely related collections where ranking is deemphasized.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://www.nngroup.com/articles/cards-component/"},{"claim":"Cards function as summary entry points, not full-detail containers — they present 'a linked entry point to further details, rather than the full details themselves,' which directly shapes how much information should be surfaced per card.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://www.nngroup.com/articles/cards-component/"},{"claim":"Card view is visually engaging and creates effective groupings, while list view allows for easy sorting and is space efficient.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://www.nngroup.com/videos/card-view-vs-list-view/"},{"claim":"CSS Grid masonry (`grid-template-rows: masonry`) is experimental in Firefox Nightly only as of May 2025 and has no cross-browser support, making it unsuitable for production card browsers.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2025/05/masonry-css-should-grid-evolve-stand-aside-new-module/"},{"claim":"The JavaScript grid hack for masonry uses `grid-auto-rows: 10px` combined with JS that calculates `gridRowEnd` spans from measured item heights, and requires recalculation on resize or content changes causing potential layout jumps.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2025/05/masonry-css-should-grid-evolve-stand-aside-new-module/"},{"claim":"The shadcn bento grid uses a six-cell layout with varied col-span and row-span values to create visual weight and hierarchy across cells.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://www.shadcn.io/blocks/hero-bento-grid"},{"claim":"The bento grid is explicitly asymmetric — columns span different widths across cards — rather than a uniform grid.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://www.shadcn.io/blocks/hero-bento-grid"},{"claim":"Larger cells are used to emphasize featured/primary content while smaller cells carry secondary information, establishing a deliberate visual hierarchy.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://www.shadcn.io/blocks/hero-bento-grid"},{"claim":"CSS-native masonry layout can be achieved with a single property — `grid-template-rows: masonry` — requiring no JavaScript.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://utilitybend.com/blog/masonry-with-css-grid-finally-a-solution-without-javascript"},{"claim":"As of the article's 2021 writing, CSS grid masonry was only available in Firefox Nightly behind a feature flag (`layout.css.grid-template-masonry-value.enabled`), not in stable browsers.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://utilitybend.com/blog/masonry-with-css-grid-finally-a-solution-without-javascript"},{"claim":"A May 2023 update to the article notes the feature is still experimental, with separate Chrome and WebKit proposals still in progress — meaning production use remained blocked years after initial proposal.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://utilitybend.com/blog/masonry-with-css-grid-finally-a-solution-without-javascript"},{"claim":"Bento layouts with more than 12-15 visible cards lose their organizational advantage; an 8-card vs 16-card test showed the 8-card version had 31% lower time-to-target-action.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://artofstyleframe.com/blog/css-layout-patterns-grid-flexbox-2026/"},{"claim":"Bento grids fail on mobile because the visual hierarchy collapses into a stack of identical-width blocks.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://artofstyleframe.com/blog/css-layout-patterns-grid-flexbox-2026/"},{"claim":"CSS grid masonry layout can be achieved natively using `grid-template-rows: masonry`, but as of January 2021 this was experimental and only available in Firefox behind a feature flag.","vote":"0-0","source":"https://griffadev.medium.com/building-a-responsive-progressively-enhanced-masonry-layout-with-only-css-and-html-998b2de89cf2"}],"sources":[{"url":"https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2025/05/masonry-css-should-grid-evolve-stand-aside-new-module/","quality":"secondary","claimCount":5},{"ur
